commentary_text: The market prices a Senegal win at 76% while the model sits at 61%—a 15-percentage-point gap favoring the favorite. The model also assigns the draw 26% versus the market's 16%, another notable spread. These gaps suggest the market has incorporated information the model cannot access, such as recent team form, injury status, or lineup composition that sharpens confidence in a Senegal result. The dashboard flags this as a signal worth noting: sportsbooks and traders may be reading developments that strengthen conviction in the favorite beyond what Elo and goal-scoring patterns alone capture.
